annotations.label: Representation as homomorphism to automorphism group

anchor.snippet: a "representation" means a homomorphism from the group to the automorphism group of an object

annotations.note: `Action.ρAut` gives the homomorphism `G →* Aut A.V` into the automorphism group of an object in an arbitrary category, exactly the general categorical notion of a representation.

annotations.label: Representation of a group

anchor.snippet: A representation of a group G on a vector space V over a field K is a group homomorphism

annotations.note: `Representation k G V := G →* V →ₗ[k] V` is the linear representation, generalized to any semiring/monoid/module, with `Representation.asGroupHom` landing in the units (GL) when `G` is a group.

annotations.label: General linear group GL(V)

anchor.snippet: GL( V ), the general linear group on V

annotations.note: `LinearMap.GeneralLinearGroup K V` is the group of invertible linear self-maps of `V`, i.e. GL(V); `Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup` is the matrix version GL(n, K).

annotations.label: Representation space, dimension, degree

anchor.snippet: Here V is called the representation space and the dimension of V is called the dimension or degree

annotations.note: The representation space is the underlying module and finite-dimensional representations are bundled as `FDRep`, but the degree/dimension is only `Module.finrank` and is not a separately named notion.

annotations.label: Finite-dimensional matrix representation

anchor.snippet: In the case where V is of finite dimension n it is common to choose a basis

annotations.note: Finite-dimensional representations exist as `FDRep`, but the basis-dependent matrix form `G →* GLₙ(K)` is not separately defined as a matrix representation.

annotations.label: Continuous representation

anchor.snippet: a continuous representation of G on V is a representation

annotations.note: `Action.IsContinuous` (giving the `ContAction` subcategory) requires the induced action `G ×  X → X` to satisfy `ContinuousSMul`, formalizing continuity of a representation.

annotations.label: Kernel of a representation

anchor.snippet: The kernel of a representation ρ of a group G is defined as the normal subgroup

annotations.note: Since `Representation` is a `MonoidHom`, its kernel is `MonoidHom.ker ρ`, which is a normal subgroup via the `MonoidHom.normal_ker` instance.

annotations.label: Faithful representation

anchor.snippet: A faithful representation is one in which the homomorphism

annotations.note: Faithfulness is just injectivity of the representation as a monoid hom (`Function.Injective ⇑ρ`, equivalently `MonoidHom.ker_eq_bot_iff`), but there is no dedicated faithful-representation predicate.

annotations.label: Map of representations (equivariant map)

anchor.snippet: Given two K vector spaces V and W , a map of two representations

annotations.note: `Representation.IntertwiningMap` is a linear map commuting with the two `G`-actions, exactly an equivariant map of representations.

annotations.label: Isomorphism of representations

anchor.snippet: the map is called an isomorphism of representations

annotations.note: `Representation.Equiv` bundles an intertwining map that is a linear isomorphism, i.e. an isomorphism of representations.

annotations.label: Representation ρ of C3

anchor.snippet: This group has a representation ρ on

annotations.note: This specific worked example of a representation of the cyclic group C₃ is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isomorphic representation σ of C3

anchor.snippet: isomorphic to the previous one, is σ given by

annotations.note: This particular example representation σ of C₃ is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Faithful representation τ of C3

anchor.snippet: The group C 3 may also be faithfully represented on

annotations.note: This specific faithful example representation τ of C₃ is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cyclic permutation matrix representation

anchor.snippet: is given by the set of cyclic permutation matrices v

annotations.note: This explicit cyclic-permutation-matrix example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Action on homogeneous degree-3 polynomials

anchor.snippet: be the space of homogeneous degree-3 polynomials over the complex numbers

annotations.note: This specific representation on homogeneous degree-3 polynomials is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Subrepresentation

anchor.snippet: A subspace W of V that is invariant under the group action is called a subrepresentation

annotations.note: `Subrepresentation ρ` is a submodule stable under the `G`-action, exactly an invariant subspace/subrepresentation.

annotations.label: Irreducible and reducible representations

anchor.snippet: then the representation is said to be irreducible

annotations.note: `Representation.IsIrreducible ρ := IsSimpleOrder (Subrepresentation ρ)` says ρ has no proper nontrivial subrepresentations.

annotations.label: Maschke decomposition into irreducibles

anchor.snippet: representations of finite groups can be decomposed into a direct sum of irreducible subrepresentations

annotations.note: Maschke's theorem (`MonoidAlgebra.Submodule.exists_isCompl`) yields the instance `IsSemisimpleRepresentation ρ` for finite groups with `|G|` invertible, i.e. a decomposition into irreducible subrepresentations.

annotations.label: Decomposability of the C3 examples

anchor.snippet: the first two representations given (ρ and σ) are both decomposable into two 1-dimensional subrepresentations

annotations.note: This explicit decomposition of the C₃ examples into 1-dimensional pieces is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Set-theoretic representation

anchor.snippet: A set-theoretic representation (also known as a group action or permutation representation ) of a group G on a set X is given by a function

annotations.note: A set-theoretic/permutation representation is precisely a `MulAction G X`, with the associated hom `MulAction.toPermHom : G →* Equiv.Perm X`.

annotations.label: ρ(g) is a bijection

anchor.snippet: the axioms for a group imply that ρ( g ) is a bijection

annotations.note: `MulAction.bijective` proves that for a group acting, each `g • ·` is a bijection on the set.

annotations.label: Permutation rep as homomorphism to symmetric group

anchor.snippet: we may equivalently define a permutation representation to be a group homomorphism from G to the symmetric group S X of X

annotations.note: `MulAction.toPermHom G X : G →* Equiv.Perm X` is the equivalent reformulation of a `MulAction` as a hom into the symmetric group of X.

annotations.label: Representation in a category as a functor

anchor.snippet: a representation of G in C is a functor from G to C

annotations.note: `Action.functorCategoryEquivalence : Action V G ≌ SingleObj G ⥤ V` identifies a representation with a functor from `G` (as a one-object category) into `V`.

annotations.label: Representation in Vect_K is a linear representation

anchor.snippet: this definition is equivalent to a linear representation

annotations.note: `Rep k G := Action (ModuleCat k) G`, and `Rep.ρ`/`Rep.of` give the equivalence between such objects and ordinary linear `Representation`s.

annotations.label: G-modules from category Ab

anchor.snippet: the objects obtained are called G -modules

annotations.note: G-modules (representations in Ab ≃ ModuleCat ℤ) are the special case `Rep ℤ G`, i.e. abelian groups with a `G`-action.

annotations.label: Representations in Top

anchor.snippet: Representations in Top are homomorphisms from G to the homeomorphism group

annotations.note: Representations in Top are `Action TopCat G`, with `Action.ρAut : G →* Aut X` (homeomorphism group) and `ContAction` enforcing continuity.

annotations.label: Projective and affine representations

anchor.snippet: Two types of representations closely related to linear representations are

annotations.note: Neither projective representations nor affine representations are defined in Mathlib's representation theory.
